text.label: Runtime-animated SDF effects on a batched UGUI canvas. The Gallery shows the library; the Playground lets you DRIVE an effect live with sliders (signal-bound, zero UnityEvents); Combos puts them on real UI. Tier-1 effects (glow, sheen, gradient, spinner, morph, ring, rainbow, juice) stay on the ONE shared NeoShape material — zero extra draw calls. Tier-2 (dissolve, holo-foil, glitch) opt into their own shader.

Drag the sliders — the glow reacts in real time. Each control publishes its value to a signal stream; a NeoSignalParamBinding on the shape remaps it onto an effect param. No UnityEvents, no glue code — the link round-trips through the spec.

Effects that wake up on input — the mobile-game feel. Hover a card for a sheen or glow, hold to add juice, click to fire sparks from the cursor, or move the mouse to drag a glow with it. Enter Play mode and try them.

Glow / Sheen / Gradient Cycle / Arc Spinner / Corner Morph / Border Pulse / Hue Shift / Transform Juice are Tier-1: they only animate fields the shared SDF shader already reads (or the RectTransform), so every card still batches into one draw call. Dissolve / Holo Foil / Glitch are Tier-2 — each its own fragment shader on a per-variant material, a deliberate batch split the cost-lint flags. The Interactive tab adds pointer triggers (hover/press), click-positioned particles and a cursor-follow glow — try them in Play mode. Run Tools → Neo UI → Setup → Create or Repair Effect Assets once to bake the Tier-2 materials.
